business.headline: Piyush Goyal says 99% of India-US trade deal talks completedSpeaking about the ongoing negotiations, Goyal said discussions between the two sides would continue from June 2 to June 4 to resolve a few remaining issues before the agreement is formally announced.

Sensex sheds 500 points, Nifty slips below 23,400 despite IT rallyThe BSE Sensex fell 508.40 points, or 0.68%, to close at 74,267.34, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 165.15 points, or 0.70%, to settle at 23,382.60.

LPG prices, solar rules, tax and more : 5 big financial changes this JuneFrom LPG cylinder prices to tax payments, June brings a fresh set of financial changes for households and businesses. Some may increase costs, while others are aimed at improving security and supporting local manufacturing.

India tightens solar import rules from June 1. Here's what it means for consumersThe government now requires certain solar projects to use only domestically manufactured solar cells, a move aimed at reducing dependence on Chinese imports and strengthening local manufacturing.

US tightens curbs on Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms operating overseasThe US' best AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places such as Malaysia despite broader US efforts to starve Chinese firms of semiconductors needed to develop critical AI capabilities, reported Reuters.

Have retail investors become the real gatekeepers of India's IPO market?The broader shift in India's equity markets from one that looked to foreign capital for validation to one anchored in domestic conviction has reached the primary market fully.

India, US begin latest round of talks to finalise details of interim trade pactThe visit, scheduled from June 1 to 4 in New Delhi, will follow recent diplomatic mobility between India and the US and build on the Indian delegation's visit to Washington in April this year.
IndiGo shares rise despite Q4 loss. What's driving the rally?IndiGo shares rose even after the airline posted a March-quarter loss. Here's why the aviation company's stock price is rising today.

Infosys, Tech Mahindra, TCS jump up to 5%; why are IT stocks rallying today?US rate-cut expectations and hopes of a rebound in technology spending are driving fresh interest in IT stocks, helping the sector outperform the broader market after months of lagging.

Sensex gains nearly 300 points, Nifty above 23,600; IndiGo jumps 5%The BSE Sensex was up 312.44 points, or 0.42%, at 75,088.18 at 9:20 am, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 86.20 points, or 0.37%, to 23,633.95.

Can a 10-year SIP really make you rich? Here's the truth you need to knowInvest a few thousand rupees every month, stay invested for 10 years, and become rich, that is the promise often attached to mutual fund SIPs. But is wealth creation really that simple, or is there more to the story?

Commercial LPG cylinders become costlier from today, Delhi price up by Rs 42Commercial LPG cylinder prices were raised in Delhi and Kolkata from June 1. The move came alongside the government's push for stronger reserves and tougher anti-hoarding checks. However, there has been no change in the price of domestic LPG cylinders used by households.

Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

Petrol, diesel, ATF export duty cut: What changes from June 1The government has cut export duties on petrol, diesel and aviation turbine fuel for the fortnight from June 1. The move follows its price-linked review mechanism, while domestic fuel excise and retail rates remain unchanged.

CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg in Mumbai, Thane, Navi MumbaiThis is the second hike in less than a month, following an earlier revision on May 14. The cumulative Rs 4 rise is expected to impact daily commuters, taxi operators and commercial vehicle users who rely heavily on CNG.

Illness, insurance and the cost of timingWhat happens when a chronic illness arrives before health insurance? One Multiple Sclerosis patient's journey highlights the challenges of getting coverage for pre-existing diseases in India.

Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

entertainment.headline: Kiara Advani on post-pregnancy scrutiny: Society expects you to be back in shape Kiara Advani said women are celebrated during pregnancy but judged after childbirth. She said the post-partum phase brings the greatest pressure as women are expected to be back in shape.

Shiva Rajkumar-With Love fame Abishan Jeevinth film commences with puja. Pics Director-actor Abishan Jeevinth teamed up with Shiva Rajkumar for a new Tamil entertainer backed by Drama Company. The film, directed by debutant Gautham Sivaraman, is currently under production in Chennai.

Ahaan Panday recreates SRK's Challa moment on Ali Abbas Zafar's set. Viral video Ahaan Panday has begun filming the overseas schedule of Ali Abbas Zafar's untitled film, as videos from London and Birmingham surface online.

Jim Sarbh recalls being mesmerised by Naseeruddin Shah at 18: This is incredible Actor Jim Sarbh recalled being awestruck by Naseeruddin Shah's stage performance at 18 and calls working with the veteran actor in Made in India – A Titan Story a dream come true.

Raakh trailer: Ali Fazal, Sonali Bendre in Ranga-Billa-inspired thriller Prime Video has unveiled the trailer of Raakh, led by Ali Fazal, Sonali Bendre and Aamir Bashir. Inspired by the Ranga-Billa case, the series frames its investigation through grief, morality and the toll on families.

Ucha Lamba Kad gets a Welcome back: Akshay, Disha revive Katrina's iconic track The new song Ucha Lamba Kad Forever from Welcome To The Jungle, featuring Akshay Kumar and Disha Patani was released on June 3. The recreated version brings back Katrina Kaif's iconic song from Welcome (2007).

Madhuri, Triptii react as video of them ignoring influencer-actor Dharna goes viral Actors Triptii Dimri and Madhuri Dixit addressed the controversy surrounding Dharna Durga during Maa Behen promotions, saying social media often creates debates out of harmless moments.

Jackky Bhagnani breaks silence on PVR Inox dues row with father Vashu Jackky Bhagnani has denied reports of legal proceedings between PVR INOX Pictures and Pooja Entertainment. He shared a statement and called the reports "entirely inaccurate."

Mari Selvaraj announces Manjanathi with Ilaiyaraaja, calls it symphony of my heart Director Mari Selvaraj has announced Manjanathi, his first film with Ilaiyaraaja, with a video filmed at the composer's home. The film formalises a collaboration the director has long described as a personal dream.

The censored, most haunting scene from Obsession you can't shake off Spoiler alert: Obsession is everywhere these days - on the internet, in newsroom discussions, in theatres, and once you watch it, in your mind as well. But there's one scene that burrows deep into your memory and refuses to leave. Once you meet Nikki and Bear, there's no way out of it.

After Obsession, Backrooms becomes 2026's biggest horror box office debut A24's horror film Backrooms opened to $81.5 million in North America and $118 million worldwide in its first weekend. The debut set a new domestic opening record for the studio and put the film on course for more box office milestones.

Obsession box office: Rs 9 crore in 4 days, horror sleeper hit leaves India hooked Obsession continues its impressive run in India after a strong weekend jump. Here's how the low-budget horror film has performed on Day 4 and why it has become a global box office sensation.

Tusshar Kapoor recalls Kareena's confidence during Mujhe Kucch Kehna Hai shoot Marking 25 years in films, Tusshar Kapoor recalled working with Kareena Kapoor in his debut film Mujhe Kucch Kehna Hai. The actor said her confidence, ease and support on set left a lasting impression on him.

Suriya gifts Rs 30 lakh-worth cars to Karuppu crew as film hits Rs 300 crore Suriya has gifted Mahindra BE6 Batman edition cars to key Karuppu team members as the film continued its box office run. The gesture has added to the buzz around the film, which has collected Rs 300 crore worldwide in three weeks.

Exclusive: Poorna Jagannathan on evolving South Asian representation in Hollywood Poorna Jagannathan said Deli Boys reflects a new phase of South Asian representation in Hollywood. In an exclusive conversation with India Today, she said identities are now embedded in stories rather than being treated as the entire plot.

Poor audio, poop-covered seats: Ilaiyaraaja fans allege concert mismanagement Music maestro Ilaiyaraaja performed his first symphony, Valiant, and a long set of hit songs over two days in Chennai. The concert drew both praise for its symphonic scale and complaints about venue arrangements amid mismanagement claims.

Mahhi Vij's farewell note gets Jay Bhanushali's support Mahhi Vij has bid farewell to her character Kausar on Sehar Hone Ko Hai. Her ex-husband Jay Bhanushali also showed his support on her farewell post.

Log velle hai: Bobby Deol shuts down fallout rumours with Alia Bhatt on Alpha set Bobby Deol set the record straight and denied reports of a fallout with Alia Bhatt during the making of Alpha. Praising her work ethic, he said much of what circulates online is inaccurate.

WATCH: Poorna Jagannathan on South Asian Representation in the West, Fred Armisen, Deli Boys In an exclusive chat with India Today's Bhavna Agarwal, Jio Hotstar's Deli Boys stars Poorna Jagannathan and Fred Armisen discuss Season 2, their unexpected on-screen chemistry, cultural discoveries on set, and the evolution of South Asian representation in Western media.

general news.headline: CBSE's silence on tender controversy is deafening. 3 questions it must answer nowThe allegations against CBSE may or may not stand the test of scrutiny. The questions raised by students certainly deserve to. Until the board directly addresses them, the Coempt tender row will remain a story about transparency as much as procurement.

PM has time to speak on mangoes: Rahul Gandhi flags 'phone-scanned' CBSE copiesCongress leader Rahul Gandhi accused CBSE and its vendor of using mobile phones to scan Class 12 answer sheets after tender norms were changed. The charge has intensified scrutiny of the board's digital evaluation system, with students and researchers raising security and procurement concerns.

Off Saudi death row, 2 decades and Rs 34 crore later: A Kerala man's storyAbdul Raheem, a Kerala native who spent two decades on Saudi Arabia's death row before returning home after a public campaign raised Rs 35 crore in blood money, speaks to IndiaToday.in about life behind bars and rebuilding his dreams.

DK Shivakumar has a big Adani dilemma amid Congress's anti-Adani RaGaAs DK Shivakumar is set to be sworn in as the Chief Minister of Karnataka, his first major governance test will be providing assent to the Adani Group's bid for the Bengaluru tunnel road project. In his attack on the Modi government, Congress leader Rahul Gandhi has regularly referred to the group, alleging crony capitalism.

If Ganga-Jamuni tehzeeb was harmony, why does Nandi still wait?For centuries, the Nandi has kept his silent vigil in Kashi, gazing towards the spot where the sanctum once stood, even as generations were taught to call it a fountain. The idea of Ganga-Jamuni tehzeeb is celebrated as a story of harmony and synthesis. If it truly is a confluence of rivers, why does Nandi still wait for his Shiva?

Zee to broadcast FIFA World Cup 2026 in India, secures 8-year dealFIFA World Cup 2026: India was one of the last major markets without a World Cup broadcaster. That changed on Monday as Zee secured rights to the 2026 tournament and a host of FIFA events through 2034.

St. Stephen's welcomes first woman principal despite DU objections to selection processSt Stephen's College is set to appoint Susan Elias as its first woman principal from June 1. The move has sharpened its autonomy dispute with Delhi University over the selection process.

60 of 80 TMC MLAs skip Mamata's meet day after attack on nephew AbhishekThe Trinamool Congress was forced to call off a legislators' meeting at Mamata Banerjee's residence after only 20 of the party's 80 newly elected MLAs attended.

No gym, only khana? Why Delhi Gymkhana order is making elite clubs sweatOccupying some of India's most valuable, government-owned real estate, elite clubs from Delhi to Mumbai are facing uncomfortable questions. The Delhi Gymkhana Club row has sparked a conversation on whether public land should continue to support private privilege. The row has left the elites sweating.

JEE Advanced AIR 1 studied just 6 hours a day: Toppers reveal what really workedJEE Advanced 2026 results are out, with Shubham Kumar securing AIR 1. Here's a closer look at the top rankers, their preparation strategies, study routines, family support systems and the advice they shared for future IIT aspirants after cracking one of India's toughest exams.

Is BJP's Annamalai launching new party in TN? Posters across Coimbatore spark buzzSupporters of Annamalai's fan welfare outfit have begun enrolling members and office-bearers, while his absence from two BJP state meetings and recent differences with the Centre over the three-language policy have intensified speculation about his next political move.

More heat, rain, flooding: These Indian cities are at high climate riskAn Azim Premji University assessment says India's coastal cities face rising heat, heavier rain, flooding and sea-level risks by 2040. The findings point to growing pressure on livelihoods, infrastructure and ecosystems, with urgent adaptation needed.

I stopped taking cab to work. Delhi Metro changed both my budget and fitnessAs someone who had grown accustomed to relying on Uber and Rapido for my daily commute, switching back to the metro wasn't the easiest adjustment. But a few days in, it became clear that the change was worth it.

Indians are buying homes with money they haven't earned yetAs home prices race ahead of incomes, more Indians are stretching EMIs into retirement, cutting savings and relying on decades of future earnings to keep the dream of homeownership alive. This is the story of how India's housing dream is increasingly being financed by tomorrow's income.

Beloved 'Parvati Aunty' dies in Delhi building collapse. She had gone back to helpParvati, known as Parvati Aunty to students and office-goers, died after debris buried her canteen in the Mehrauli building collapse. Her death was mourned by residents of the area, where her small eatery had become a daily refuge for many.

26-year-old Delhi model goes swimming in 'death trap' Kerala quarry, drownsDivyanshu Joshi, who had gone to the abandoned quarry to scout the location for an ad shoot, drowned after entering the water. The panchayat said the site had long been treated as dangerous and no permission had been granted for the shoot.

From Obsession to Companion: Hollywood is entering its 'incel horror' eraObsession centres on Bear who uses the 'One Wish Willow' to force Nikki's love. Its viral Hansel and Gretel monologue, among other aspects, has sharpened debate around consent, control and male entitlement in modern horror.

Harsh Goenka shares 2019 Swiss hotel rules for Indians amid civic sense debateHarsh Goenka linked viral videos of Indians abroad to a broader lack of civic sense. His remarks revived memories of a Swiss hotel's special rules for Indian guests and renewed debate on public behaviour overseas.

Ate golgappas, ice cream for free: UP cop suspended after shopkeepers' complaintKanpur Police suspended a PRV constable after traders alleged he took free food from vendors and a video surfaced online. A detailed inquiry will now determine whether further departmental or legal action follows.

How father's tales of a helmetless Gavaskar forged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's gritOn a historic IPL presentation night, 15-year-old MVP Vaibhav Sooryavanshi bypassed the usual corporate scripts to recall how his father's backyard stories of a helmetless Sunil Gavaskar forged his fearless approach to the game. He also made it clear that he wants to play Test cricket for India, sooner rather than later.

Hours after wedding, Indian-origin pilot killed in US chopper crash, wife survivesHours after saying "I do", Dave Fiji was killed in a helicopter crash in Georgia's Dawson County and his bride Jessni was injured. The accident has left his parents, who emigrated from Kerala, grieving.

Monsoon arrival pushed back again: When will rains reach India?The season that millions have been waiting for is once again facing an unexpected hurdle, extending the wait for relief from one of India's hottest years.

45-litre tank, 52 litres of petrol: Kanpur car owner alleges fuel pump scamIn a video, the car owner claimed that the petrol pump billed him for 52 litres of petrol in a 45-litre tank, prompting him to urge the local administration to investigate the alleged scam.

I took a year off while everyone moved forward: What I learnt along the wayA 31-year-old media professional recounts leaving work for a year after burnout. The break reshaped the writer's relationship with work, rest and ambition.

sports.headline: RCB's dressing room is the biggest prize, not the IPL Andy Flower's tribute to Virat Kohli, Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood offered a glimpse into the culture behind RCB's back-to-back IPL titles, where leadership, trust and selflessness matter as much as talent.

PV Sindhu congratulates RCB on IPL glory, adds cheeky lucky charm remark PV Sindhu congratulated Royal Challengers Bengaluru after their successful IPL title defence and joked that her move to Bangalore has coincided with their winning run. On Sunday, RCB beat Gujarat Titans in the final at the Narendra Modi Stadium.

Zee to broadcast FIFA World Cup 2026 in India, secures 8-year deal FIFA World Cup 2026: India was one of the last major markets without a World Cup broadcaster. That changed on Monday as Zee secured rights to the 2026 tournament and a host of FIFA events through 2034.

Senegal AFCON walkout sparks major law changes ahead of FIFA World Cup 2026 FIFA World Cup: IFAB has approved sweeping law changes after AFCON 2026 flashpoints, including Senegal's final walkout and tougher sanctions for protest and confrontation. The measures will apply from 2026-27 and at World Cup 2026, signalling a harder line on dissent, delay and discriminatory conduct.

RCB's mane attraction: Rajat Patidar, like MS Dhoni is making calm the new cool again IPL 2026: The long hair may invite comparisons to MS Dhoni, but Rajat Patidar's calm leadership, trust in players and ability to stay composed under pressure are what truly make RCB's captain resemble Captain Cool.

RCB unlikely to hold victory parade in Bengaluru after IPL title win RCB fans are unlikely to get a victory parade after the team's IPL title defence, with the franchise opting against public celebrations in Bengaluru amid safety concerns and strict police guidelines following last year's deadly stampede.

Secretly been working with Sat-Chi: Ex-Danish shuttler after Singapore Open win Mathias Boe has revealed he quietly rejoined Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty as a consultant before their Singapore Open win. The move restores a proven tactical alliance as the pair build towards the world championships in Delhi and the Asian Games in Japan.

Rajat Patidar not done yet, wants RCB to script IPL hat-trick history Rajat Patidar said he wants to help Royal Challengers Bengaluru achieve an unprecedented hat-trick of IPL titles. On Sunday, RCB became only the third franchise in tournament history to win back-to-back championships, but no team has ever gone on to win three titles in a row.

Luis Suarez left out of Uruguay World Cup squad as Valverde, Nunez make cut FIFA World Cup: Marcelo Bielsa left Luis Suarez and Nahitan Nandez out of Uruguay's final 26-man World Cup squad. The decision reinforces his youth-first, high-intensity reset despite Suarez's attempted return and recent criticism.

I'll remember IPL 2026 for Sooryavanshi: Sunil Gavaskar after RCB vs GT final Sunil Gavaskar said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's six-hitting and powerplay run surge defined IPL 2026 for him. The praise underlined how a 15-year-old's fearless season has shifted the conversation to Indian cricket's next batting force.

Day 9 Schedule: Aryna Sabalenka, Naomi Osaka face off in blockbuster clash French Open 2026 Day 9: Aryna Sabalenka and Naomi Osaka are set to square off in a blockbuster fourth-round clash during the night session, while India's N. Sriram Balaji, Maaya Rajeshwaran Revathi and Aryan Paparkar will also be in action at Roland-Garros.

French Open ushers in new faces beyond Alcaraz, Sinner as U21 trio makes historyFrench Open 2026: Joao Fonseca, Jakub Mensík, and Rafael Jodar scripted history as three players aged 21 or under reached a Grand Slam quarterfinal for the first time this century.

RCB's Lakers era: A dynasty in the making, three-peat in sightIPL 2026: After an 18-year wait for their maiden title, RCB now have back-to-back IPL crowns. With a winning culture and long-term vision taking shape, are they building the IPL's next great dynasty, much like the LA Lakers in the NBA?

RCB's back-to-back titles built on foundations set by IPL mega-auctionIPL 2026: RCB's back-to-back IPL titles weren't won on glamour, but engineered via mega-auction squad depth. By replacing individual reliance with quality backups, they built a bulletproof champion empire.

How father's tales of a helmetless Gavaskar forged Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's gritOn a historic IPL presentation night, 15-year-old MVP Vaibhav Sooryavanshi bypassed the usual corporate scripts to recall how his father's backyard stories of a helmetless Sunil Gavaskar forged his fearless approach to the game. He also made it clear that he wants to play Test cricket for India, sooner rather than later.

RCB rack up back-to-back IPL titles. Here's how CSK congratulated the champsIPL 2026 Final, RCB vs GT: Chennai Super Kings congratulated Royal Challengers Bengaluru with a special message after RCB secured their second successive IPL title with a five-wicket victory over Gujarat Titans at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma, Rajat Patidar: The IPL's most unlikely trilogyIPL 2026 final: He came as a replacement player, went unsold at auction, and once postponed his own wedding for a tournament that had seemingly forgotten him. Two IPL titles later, Rajat Patidar has joined the most exclusive club in Indian cricket, entirely on his own terms.

GT players evacuated as short circuit causes team bus breakdown after IPL finalIPL final: Gujarat Titans players and support staff were evacuated from their team bus after a short circuit caused smoke to spread inside the vehicle following the IPL 2026 final in Ahmedabad. All members were safe, with an alternate bus arranged to take them to the hotel.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli sets the tone, then finishes off in styleIPL final, Play of the Day: RCB beat Gujarat Titans to win their second Indian Premier League trophy. RCB joined a rare club on the back of Virat Kohli's terrific innings at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Rajat Patidar dedicates RCB's IPL title defence to Bengaluru stampede victimsRajat Patidar dedicated RCB's second successive IPL title to the 11 supporters who lost their lives in last year's Bengaluru stampede, linking the franchise's latest triumph to a season-long effort to honour the victims and their families.
After 19 years, Virat Kohli lives out his IPL dream17 years of waiting and then RCB win back to back IPL titles. But on Sunday, May 31, Virat Kohli realised his long awaited dream.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi sweeps IPL awards, makes a scary statement at the endVaibhav Sooryavanshi signed off a memorable IPL 2026 season with a clean sweep of the major individual awards, but the Rajasthan Royals teenager insisted there is still plenty of room for improvement.

Winning six, flying kiss: Kohli-Anushka moments go viral after RCB title winVirat Kohli's six sealed Royal Challengers Bengaluru's five-wicket win over Gujarat Titans before he blew a flying kiss towards Anushka Sharma in the stands. RCB won their second title on the trot.

RCB's champion's mindset is making Virat's job easier: Don't have to do it aloneVirat Kohli attributed Royal Challengers Bengaluru's second successive IPL crown to the squad's depth and collective strength, admitting he no longer feels solely responsible for the team's success.

18 years for one. 12 months for another. RCB are IPL champions, againEighteen years to win the first. Twelve months to win the second. Royal Challengers Bengaluru steamrolled Gujarat Titans in the IPL 2026 final to cement their place among cricket's most ruthless dynasties.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi enjoys IPL 2026 final with Jay Shah, sports new haircutVaibhav Sooryavanshi was spotted at the IPL 2026 final in Ahmedabad alongside Jay Shah. He arrived to collect major season awards after a record-breaking campaign and sported a new haircut as well.

French Open: Zverev through to quarters, teenage Jodar continues impressive runSecond seed Alexander Zverev moved into the French Open quarter-finals with a 7-6(3), 6-4, 6-1 win over lucky loser Jesper de Jong on Sunday, setting up a last-eight meeting with Spanish teenager Rafael Jodar.

How RCB's bowling plan ripped through Gujarat Titans in the IPL finalRoyal Challengers Bengaluru's pace attack made full use of a sluggish Ahmedabad surface, restricting Gujarat Titans to a below-par 155 for 8 in the IPL 2026 final at the Narendra Modi Stadium on Sunday.

Orange Cap at 15: Sooryavanshi eclipses Shubman Gill to finish IPL 2026 on topVaibhav Sooryavanshi finished IPL 2026 with the Orange Cap after Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan fell early in the final against RCB. The 15-year-old's 776-run season also made him the youngest winner of the accolade.

Be fair to GT: Gavaskar says IPL final should've been delayed after travel chaosSunil Gavaskar said the IPL 2026 final should have been moved to the reserve day after Gujarat Titans were hit by a travel delay. He argued the disruption left GT mentally and physically drained, making the scheduling unfair.

Ruturaj replaces Parag, named India A vice-captain for Sri Lanka tri-seriesRuturaj Gaikwad has replaced the injured Riyan Parag in the India A squad for the Sri Lanka tri-series and will serve as vice-captain. The call gives Gaikwad a chance to reset after a lean IPL while Parag moves to the Centre of Excellence for rehabilitation.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
